comprehensive_summary:
Luigi Pirandello is an Italian Nobel Prize-winning playwright and novelist best known for his metatheatrical play Six Characters in Search of an Author, his play Henry IV about a man who believes himself to be a Holy Roman Emperor, and his novel The Late Mattia Pascal.

Six Characters in Search of an Author is Pirandello's most frequently clued work by a wide margin. Six characters -- the Father, the Mother, the Son, the Step-Daughter, the Boy, and the Child -- interrupt a rehearsal of Pirandello's own play Mixing It Up (also called The Rules of the Game or Il giuoco delle parti). The Father claims to be possessed by a 'Demon of Experiment' and begs the Stage Manager to let his family tell their story. The hanging of coats and hats on a rack summons Madame Pace, who runs a brothel/fashion house where the Step-Daughter works as a prostitute. The Father offers a hundred lire in a 'pale blue envelope' and flirts with his former step-daughter in the brothel. The Step-Daughter performs the cabaret number 'Prenez garde a Tchou-Tchin-Tchou.' A girl dressed in white with a black sash clings to a woman whose 'wax-like' face is covered by a widow's veil. The play ends when the Child drowns in a fountain and the Boy shoots himself, leading the Stage Manager to exclaim 'I've lost a whole day over these people.' The Leading Man complains about wearing a chef's hat. The curtain is meant to be left up between acts. The Prompter reads out 'When the curtain rises, Leo Gala is busy beating an egg in a cup.' The play is part of Pirandello's 'theatre in the theatre' trilogy, along with Each in His Own Way and Tonight We Improvise.

Henry IV (Enrico IV) is about a nobleman who falls off his horse during a costume pageant/masquerade and comes to believe he is the Holy Roman Emperor Henry IV. Count di Nolli pays four valets to serve him in a residence at Goslar for eleven years. A portrait of Matilda dressed as a famous countess hangs in his 'throne room.' A doctor attempts to 'cure' him by replacing two portraits, the only modern objects visible. The title character stabs Tito Belcredi (whom he mistakes for Peter Damian) after the attempted cure. He chides servants for not living in a dream of history. He relates his situation to a sleeping Irish priest being touched by a flower. He develops a taste for 'playing the madman' after twenty years.

The Late Mattia Pascal is a novel about a man who wins at Monte Carlo and then learns he has been declared dead (his family mistakes another corpse for him). He adopts the name Adriano Meis and starts a new life. The librarian Don Eligio encourages him to write his story. The title character complains about Copernicus. He eventually abandons his new identity by faking his death but is unable to return to his old life.

Right You Are (If You Think So) features Laudisi investigating conflicting claims by Lady Frola and Mr. Ponza about whether Ponza's second wife is actually Frola's daughter. Laudisi looks in the mirror and asks 'Who is insane among us?' One character claims she is 'a daughter and a second wife, but nobody to herself.'

Tonight We Improvise features Doctor Hinkfuss, who fails to provide a script and leads actors in improvised performance of the novella Leonora, Addio! Pirandello's muse Marta Abba adapted it. An aviation officer leaves his wife, causing her to die while singing opera. At the end, Hinkfuss apologizes.

Other works include Liola (a rural comedy about a rake, the first play in his collection Naked Masks), Each in His Own Way (in which Francesco and Doro nearly duel over their differing interpretations of the love affairs of Delia Morello), The Man with a Flower in His Mouth (a one-act dialogue on Christmas Eve between a man who missed his train and a man dying of facial epithelioma -- the first TV drama produced by the BBC in 1930), The Old and the Young (a historical novel set in Sicily after the Garibaldi uprising of 1860), and 'The Dissolution of the League' (about Mafia cattle thievery). His essay 'On Humor' examined Don Abbondio in Manzoni's The Betrothed.

Pirandello is a Sicilian author. Dario Fo was the first Italian to win the Nobel Prize since Pirandello.

works.name: Six Characters in Search of an Author
works.description: Six Characters in Search of an Author is Pirandello's most famous metatheatrical play. Six characters interrupt a rehearsal of Mixing It Up, including the Father, the Step-Daughter, and the Mother. Madame Pace is summoned by hanging coats. The play ends with the Child drowning in a fountain and the Boy shooting himself. The Stage Manager exclaims 'I've lost a whole day over these people.'

clues.clue: Six characters (Father, Mother, Step-Daughter, etc.) interrupt a rehearsal of Mixing It Up / The Rules of the Game
quotes: a motley crew rehearses Mixing It Up
a family's entrance interrupts a rehearsal of The Rules of the Game

clues.clue: Hanging coats/hats on a rack summons Madame Pace, who runs a brothel where the Step-Daughter works
quotes: coats and hats being hung on a rack attracts Madame Pace to the stage
the hanging of coats summons Madame Pace, who hires the Step-daughter as a prostitute

clues.clue: Ends with the Child drowning in a fountain and the Boy shooting himself; Stage Manager exclaims 'I've lost a whole day over these people'
quotes: the deaths of the Child and the Boy lead the Stage Manager to complain, 'I've lost a whole day over these people'
a child shoots himself behind a tree when a baby is discovered drowned in a fountain

clues.clue: The Father claims to embody a 'Demon of Experiment'; offers hundred lire in a 'pale blue envelope'
quotes: one of whom claims to be possessed by a 'Demon of Experiment'
is accused of offering a hundred lire in a 'pale blue envelope'

clues.clue: Step-Daughter performs cabaret number 'Prenez garde a Tchou-Tchin-Tchou'; is a two-months orphan
quotes: she is a two-months orphan, then performs a sing-and-dance routine of Prenez garde a Tchou-Tchin-Tchou

clues.clue: Leading Man complains about wearing a chef's hat; Prompter reads 'Leo Gala is busy beating an egg'
quotes: The Leading Man complains about wearing a chef's hat
reads out of a book, 'When the curtain rises, Leo Gala... is busy beating an egg in a cup'

clues.clue: Part of Pirandello's 'theatre in the theatre' trilogy with Each in His Own Way and Tonight We Improvise
quotes: Six Characters in Search of an Author, Tonight We Improvise, and this third play make up Pirandello's 'theatre in the theatre' trilogy

works.name: Henry IV (Enrico IV)
works.description: Henry IV is about a nobleman who falls off his horse during a masquerade and comes to believe he is the Holy Roman Emperor Henry IV. Count di Nolli pays four valets to serve him for eleven years. A doctor tries to 'cure' him by replacing two portraits. The title character stabs Tito Belcredi after the cure attempt.

clues.clue: A nobleman falls off his horse during a costume pageant and believes himself to be Holy Roman Emperor Henry IV
quotes: an aristocrat who falls off his horse and believes himself to be Henry IV
a man hits himself on the head after falling off of a horse during a pageant and believes himself to be the title Holy Roman Emperor

clues.clue: Count di Nolli pays four valets to serve him for eleven years; house turned into impression of Goslar
quotes: four valets who are instructed by Count di Nolli to serve their master for eleven years
a nobleman whose house is turned into an impression of a residence at Goslar

clues.clue: Title character stabs Tito Belcredi (mistaken for Peter Damian) after a doctor attempts to 'cure' him by replacing two portraits
quotes: the title character stabs Tito Belcredi after a doctor attempts to 'cure' him
The doctor plots to shock Henry out of his madness by replacing two of these things

clues.clue: Portrait of Matilda dressed as a famous countess hangs in his 'throne room'; he remembers her dark hair
quotes: one character remembers Matilda's dark hair due to a portrait of her dressed as a famous countess in his 'throne room'

clues.clue: Develops a taste for 'playing the madman' after twenty years of pageantry
quotes: A character created by this author develops a taste for 'playing the madman' after twenty years of royal pageantry.

clues.clue: Relates situation to a sleeping Irish priest being touched on the neck by a flower; his four 'private counselors' (Lolo, Momo, etc.) betray him
quotes: relates his situation to a sleeping Irish priest being touched on the neck by a flower after learning that his four 'private counselors,' such as Lolo and Momo, have betrayed him

works.name: The Late Mattia Pascal
works.description: The Late Mattia Pascal is a novel about a man who wins at Monte Carlo and is declared dead when his family mistakes another corpse for him. He adopts the name Adriano Meis. The librarian Don Eligio encourages him to write his story. He complains about Copernicus. He eventually cannot return to his old life.

clues.clue: Title character uses Monte Carlo winnings to pose as Adriano Meis after being declared dead; family mistakes another corpse for him
quotes: a character uses his winnings from Monte Carlo to pose as Adriano Meis after he is declared dead
his wife and mother-in-law mistake another man's corpse for his own

clues.clue: Don Eligio the librarian encourages the title character to write his story; he complains about Copernicus
quotes: The title character of a novel by this author complains about Copernicus to the librarian who encourages him to write his story, Don Eligio.

clues.clue: Eventually abandons his new identity by faking death but cannot return to old life
quotes: He eventually abandons that identity by faking his death, only to be unable to return to his old life.

works.name: Right You Are (If You Think So)
works.description: Right You Are features Laudisi investigating conflicting claims by Lady Frola and Mr. Ponza about whether Ponza's second wife is Frola's daughter. Laudisi looks in the mirror and asks 'Who is insane among us?' One character says she is 'a daughter and a second wife, but nobody to herself.'

quotes: Lady Frola and Mr. Ponza both accuse each other of insanity when they give conflicting accounts
Laudisi's attempts to discover whether Ponza or Signora Frola was telling the truth

clues.clue: Laudisi looks in mirror and asks 'Who is insane among us?'; a woman claims to be 'a daughter and a second wife, but nobody to herself'
quotes: an investigator looks in the mirror and asks, 'Who is insane among us?'
a woman claims that she is a daughter and a second wife, but nobody to herself

clues.clue: Mother allowed to visit daughter only by shouting up to a balcony five stories above
quotes: a mother who is only allowed to visit her daughter by shouting up to her on a balcony five stories above

works.name: Other Works
works.description: Tonight We Improvise features Doctor Hinkfuss leading actors without a script; Marta Abba adapted it. Each in His Own Way has Francesco and Doro nearly dueling over Delia Morello's love affairs. Liola is a rural comedy about a rake. The Man with a Flower in His Mouth is a one-act dialogue (first BBC TV drama, 1930). The Old and the Young is set in post-Garibaldi Sicily. 'On Humor' examined Manzoni's The Betrothed.

clues.clue: Tonight We Improvise: Doctor Hinkfuss fails to provide a script; actors perform Leonora, Addio!; Hinkfuss apologizes at end
quotes: Doctor Hinkfuss' attempts to get actors to perform the title action
Doctor Hinkfuss fails to provide a script

clues.clue: Each in His Own Way: Francesco and Doro nearly duel over Delia Morello's love affairs
quotes: Francesco and Doro nearly duel over their differing interpretations of the love affairs of Delia Morello

clues.clue: Liola: rural comedy about a rake; first play in collection Naked Masks; opens with peasant girls shelling almonds
quotes: peasant girls singing hymns and shelling almonds before the jolly title character enters with his three boys mothered by three different women

clues.clue: The Man with a Flower in His Mouth: a one-act dialogue between a man who missed his train and a man dying of facial epithelioma; first BBC TV drama (1930)
quotes: a one-act dialogue between a businessman who has missed his train and a character dying of an epithelioma
A 1930 production of this play was the first piece of television drama produced by the BBC

clues.clue: The Old and the Young: historical novel set in Sicily after Garibaldi uprising of 1860
quotes: This historical novel by Pirandello is set in Sicily following the Garibaldi uprising of 1860

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: Pirandello is identified as an Italian Nobel Prize-winning playwright and absurdist. He is Sicilian. He was a precursor to the Theatre of the Absurd. Dario Fo was the first Italian to win the Nobel Prize since Pirandello. His essay 'On Humor' examined Manzoni's The Betrothed. His muse was Marta Abba.

clues.clue: Italian playwright of Six Characters in Search of an Author; Nobel Prize winner
quotes: name this Italian playwright of Six Characters in Search of an Author
this Italian Nobel Laureate

clues.clue: Dario Fo was the first Italian Nobel laureate since Pirandello
quotes: Dario Fo won the Nobel Prize in Literature in 1997, becoming the first Italian to win it since this author
Dario Fo received the Nobel Prize in Literature for his plays, much like this earlier Italian playwright

clues.clue: Sicilian author; wrote about Mafia cattle thievery in 'The Dissolution of the League'
quotes: This author wrote about Mafia thievery of cattle in 'The Dissolution of the League'

clues.clue: His essay 'On Humor' examined Don Abbondio in Manzoni's The Betrothed
quotes: This author's essay 'On Humor' examined the comic relief Don Abbondio provides in The Betrothed.
